data.title: ASML workforce escalates protest against 1,700 job cuts in second mass walkout
Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses EU rollout slowed by battery, AI rules and supply constraints: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms' (META) rollout of new display-equipped Ray-Ban smart glasses in the European Union has reportedly been hampered by battery and AI regulations in addition to supply constraints.

The social networking giant wants to launch the product in the EU but has been unable to secure enough supply, Bloomberg reported. Meta is also in discussions with the EU about the battery law and is seeking a carve-out for smart glasses developed both by it and other companies, the report added.

The EU's batteries regulation requires that from February 18, 2027 portable batteries in devices sold on the EU market be readily removed. Meta's smart glasses do not have removable batteries.

Meta (META) earlier this year said it has decided to pause international expansion of its Ray-Ban Display glasses due to short supply and strong demand in the United States. The Facebook parent had planned to roll out its augmented reality smart glasses, Meta Ray-Ban Display, in the UK, France, Italy, and Canada starting early this year after tasting success with earlier versions.

The Ray-Ban Meta glasses, developed with EssilorLuxottica's (ESLOF) Ray-Ban, let wearers take photos, stream content, and talk to an AI assistant. 

A New York Times report last month described the company’s internal deliberations on adding facial recognition capabilities to its popular smart glasses. The technology could come as soon as this year, the paper reported.

data.title: Anduril, Palantir work on software for Golden Dome shield - report
data.description:
Anduril Industries and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) are working together to develop the core software for the U.S. “Golden Dome” missile‑defense initiative, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

These companies are part of a broader consortium of defense and technology firms aiming to have the Golden Dome software ready for testing as early as this summer. Other companies involved are Aalyria Technologies, AI startup Scale AI; and software firm Swoop Technologies, according to the report.

The companies will be collaborating to build the core software layer of the Golden Dome project—an $185B initiative to build a space-based missile shield designed to counter ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic threats.

Anduril Industries is known for autonomous defense systems and AI-driven surveillance platforms. Their expertise lies in sensor fusion, autonomous drones, and battlefield AI. Its founder, Palmer Luckey, had earlier said that he doesn't object to the tighter oversight of defense contractors proposed by President Donald Trump, including potential limits on executive compensation.

Palantir Technologies specializes in data integration, analytics, and decision-support software. The company could likely focus on real-time threat detection, tracking, and command-and-control systems. Recently, Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve ​Feinberg reportedly told Pentagon leaders that Palantir’s (PLTR) Maven AI system will become an official program of record.

Space Force Gen. Michael Guetlein, who leads the project at the Pentagon, described the software as a “glue layer” designed to help military officers coordinate and manage networks of sensors, radars, and missile systems.

After the launch of the Golden Dome software consortium last year, Michael Guetlein brought in established defense contractors, including Lockheed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC), and RTX (RTX), to support development of the system’s operating framework.

PLTR (PLTR) stock traded marginally higher to $156.70 on Wednesday during pre-market hours.

data.title: Intrusion anticipates revenue recognition in H1 2026 as contract delays impact Q4 2025 earnings
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Intrusion Inc. (INTZ) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Anthony Scott highlighted that fiscal year 2025 began and ended with unexpected events, notably the full elimination of debt and Series A preferred stock at the start of the year, and an unanticipated delay in the extension of a critical infrastructure contract with the Department of War at year-end. Scott emphasized, "At the beginning of the year, we improved our balance sheet by fully eliminating our then outstanding debt and Series A preferred stock."
  • Product milestones included launching critical infrastructure solutions for essential assets, making two versions of Shield Cloud available on AWS marketplace, and introducing Shield Cloud on Microsoft's Azure platform. Scott stated, "With availability across both leading cloud marketplaces, we've meaningfully expanded our sales reach, which will help enhance our customer pipeline and drive future revenue growth."
  • Strategic partnerships expanded, with PortNexus embedding Intrusion's Shield endpoint solution in MyFlare safety technology and the launch of the P.O.S.S.E program for law enforcement cyber protection. Scott noted, "Our pilot experience with the P.O.S.S.E program is encouraging with a high adoption rate so far."
  • Management changes included hiring Valencia Reaves as Public Sector Vice President of Sales and Patrick Duggan as Director of Channel Sales & Partnerships to strengthen business development efforts.
  • CFO Kimberly Pinson reported, "Fourth quarter results totaled $1.5 million in revenue, a decrease of 25%, compared to the prior quarter, and 12% when compared to the prior year period, as noted earlier on the call. This was due to the delayed incremental funding of a major U.S. government contract."

Outlook

  • Management anticipates recognizing delayed revenue from the Department of War contract extension in the first half of 2026, assuming normalization of procurement activity. Scott stated, "We do believe that we will be able to recognize this revenue during the first half of 2026 once procurement activity normalizes."
  • The company expects the sale of OT Defender and expanded Shield offerings to contribute to future growth, with a focus on both commercial and government sectors. Pinson stated that the expanded pipeline for Shield technology, especially through PortNexus, is "expected to support future Shield revenue growth."
  • Additional small debt financing is planned to support growth initiatives.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $1.5 million, down 25% sequentially and 12% year-over-year, attributed to the contract delay. Consulting revenues totaled $1.1 million, while Shield revenues reached $0.4 million.
  • Gross profit margin for Q4 stood at 74%, slightly down from the prior year period.
  • Operating expenses for Q4 were $4 million, reflecting a $0.3 million sequential and $0.8 million year-over-year increase, driven by higher sales and marketing investments.
  • Net loss for Q4 was $2.8 million or $0.14 per share, compared to a net loss of $2 million in Q4 2024. Full-year net loss totaled $9.1 million or $0.46 per share.
  • Cash and cash equivalents were $3.6 million at year-end, with the company planning to seek additional debt financing.

Q&A

  • Scott Buck, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about average contract value and sales cycles for the P.O.S.S.E program. CEO Scott responded that device pricing varies by department size and bandwidth needs, ranging from a few thousand to tens of thousands of dollars, and highlighted a high adoption rate and quick sales cycles once potential clients see product performance.
  • Buck also queried the impact of delayed government contracts on revenue. CEO Scott confirmed, "Yes, we were expecting to report growth, both for the quarter and -- quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year above and beyond what we reported on the year-on-year."
  • Buck asked about rising sales and marketing expenses. Pinson replied, "We will continue to invest in sales and marketing ... we will see some increases from here." Scott added plans to seek cost efficiencies elsewhere to offset increased sales and marketing spend.
  • Edward Woo, Ascendiant Capital: Asked if expenses for the delayed contract had already been recognized. Scott replied, "That's correct. We've taken all the expenses associated with that. We just are not able to recognize the revenue at this point."
  • Woo inquired about commercial pipeline delays, to which Scott stated, "Beyond the government sector, no real change."
  • Howard Brous asked about satisfaction and expansion potential for critical infrastructure customers, with Scott sharing positive feedback and highlighting expansion opportunities in the Asia-Pac region and domestically.
  • Brous also explored the PortNexus school safety solution, with Scott emphasizing high margins and strong reception, stating, "It's per classroom. ... For us, it's very high, yes, because we don't actually have to go do any install or anything."
  • An unknown analyst questioned breakeven timing. Scott said, "I would hope so, yes" regarding breakeven by Q3 2026, noting disappointment at not achieving breakeven sooner.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for specifics on contract delays, sales cycle expectations, margins, and breakeven timing, reflecting a slightly skeptical and probing sentiment, especially around government contract timing and cost management.
  • Management acknowledged setbacks but maintained an optimistic tone about future revenue recognition and product adoption. Scott frequently used phrases such as "we're confident" and "we believe we're on track," but also admitted to disappointment regarding delayed breakeven and contract extension.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the current call showed a more cautious and defensive tone from management due to the impact of contract delays and increased losses. Analysts were more persistent in their questioning about financial impacts and breakeven expectations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The fourth quarter saw a reversal from the prior quarter’s sequential revenue growth, with Q4 revenue dropping due to delayed government contract funding. This contrasts with Q3's report of six consecutive quarters of sequential topline growth.
  • Operating expenses increased in Q4 due to higher sales and marketing investments, as opposed to Q3’s more modest expense increase.
  • Management shifted from highlighting consistent growth and optimism about government contracts in Q3 to addressing the negative impact of contract delays and acknowledging disappointment in Q4.
  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more on the effects of contract delays, cost controls, and timeline to profitability, whereas Q3 questions centered on product expansion and channel partnerships.
  • Strategic priorities have shifted toward accelerating sales and marketing and preparing for revenue recognition tied to contract execution in the coming quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • The primary challenge highlighted was the delayed extension and funding of a major U.S. government contract, driven by government shutdowns and administrative constraints.
  • Management’s mitigation strategy includes continued support for deployed solutions, expanding commercial sales efforts, and seeking cost efficiencies in other areas to offset increased sales and marketing spend.
  • Analysts expressed concern over the timing and certainty of revenue recognition, potential further delays, and the path to breakeven operations.

Final Takeaway

Intrusion’s management remains confident that recent investments in product development, sales capability, and strategic partnerships will position the company for growth and eventual profitability, despite near-term setbacks from unforeseen government contract delays. The leadership underscores their belief that revenue from delayed contracts will materialize in the first half of 2026, and expresses optimism in expanding opportunities with both government and commercial clients, supported by new product offerings and expanded sales initiatives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX may file for U.S. IPO as early as this week, report says
data.description:
• SpaceX (SPACE) is aiming ​to file its initial ‌public offering prospectus with regulators later this week ​or next week, ​The Information reported on Tuesday.
  • Advisers involved in the preparation predict the company could ​try to raise more ​than $75 billion in the IPO, ‌the ⁠report said, citing a person ​with direct knowledge of the ​plans.
  • The individual investor portion might exceed 20%, but the percentage has ​yet ​to ⁠be finalized, as per the report.
  • In February, Bloomberg reported that SpaceX was considering a dual listing to give CEO Elon Musk greater control. A dual listing would enable Musk to maintain control of the company, mirroring his majority control over Tesla (TSLA) despite owning a minority stake in the company.

data.title: Meta Platforms raises top executive pay with stock options amid intensifying AI race
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is granting stock options to its top executives for the first time since its 2012 IPO, aiming to retain and reward leadership as it continues heavy spending to stay competitive in the intensifying AI race.

The new options, outlined in company filings on Tuesday, will be released if Meta hits ambitious stock-price milestones over the coming years.

CFO Susan Li, CTO Andrew Bosworth, Chief product officer Chris Cox, COO Javier Olivan, president Dina Powell McCormick, and chief legal officer Curtis Mahoney are eligible for the stock options, regulatory filings showed. CEO Mark Zuckerberg, with a net worth of over $200 billion, is not part of the plan.

All executives except McCormick and Mahoney, who joined Meta (META) in January, will also receive an increase in restricted stock awards, worth a total of $170 million at last close, which vest quarterly. Chief Accounting Officer Aaron Anderson will only receive restricted stock.

For the first tranche of options to be paid out, Meta’s stock has to hit $1,116.08, an 88% increase from Tuesday’s closing price and equal to a market cap of about $2.82 trillion, based on current shares outstanding.

The next tranche requires a stock price of $1,393.87. The price goes up significantly for each additional tranche, with the highest being $3,727.12, which would make the company worth over $9 trillion. The most valuable company in the world today is Nvidia (NVDA).

The Facebook parent's stock price is down about 4% in the past year, trailing all of its megacap tech peers apart from Microsoft (MSFT), which has dropped 5%. Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), meanwhile, has jumped 73%, boosted by the success of its Gemini AI portfolio.

The company has also offered huge pay packages, some worth hundreds of millions ​of dollars over the years, to court top AI researchers to a new superintelligence team.

data.title: Merck close to $6B acquisition of Terns Pharma to boost cancer portfolio: FT
data.description:
Merck & Co. (MRK) is close to an approximately $6 billion all-cash deal to buy US biotech firm Terns Pharmaceuticals (TERN) as it seeks to boost its drug pipeline ahead of cancer treatment Keytruda’s patent expiry, the Financial Times reported.

The talks are at an advanced stage and could be finalized in the coming days, the FT reported, citing people it didn’t identify. Terns (TERN) had a $5.4 billion market capitalization as of Tuesday’s close.

Merck and Terns didn’t immediately respond to requests for comment from the FT.

Following ⁠the news, shares of Terns Pharma, which focuses on developing treatments for ⁠chronic ​myeloid leukemia, rose about ​10% in after-hours trading.

Merck (MRK) has been pursuing deals to strengthen its pipeline, with CEO Rob Davis spearheading a strategy to diversify the company's portfolio ahead of the 2028 patent expiration for its blockbuster cancer drug, Keytruda.

data.title: Wednesday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The Mortgage Bankers' Association compiles various mortgage loan indexes.  The purchase applications index measures applications at mortgage lenders.

8:30 AM Import and Export Prices

Import price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods that are bought in the United States but produced abroad, and export price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods sold abroad but produced domestically

8:30 AM Current Account 

The current account, on a quarterly basis, measures the U.S. international balance in goods and services trade as well as unilateral transfers. 

8:30 AM Retail Inventories (Advance)

Retail inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by retailers.

8:30 AM Wholesale Inventories (Advance)

Wholesale inventories measure the monthly dollar value of inventories held by merchant wholesalers and are tracked to gauge inventory change in quarterly GDP.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Status Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on petroleum inventories in the U.S., whether produced here or abroad.

11:00 AM Survey of Business Uncertainty

The survey of business uncertainty is a panel survey measuring year-ahead expectations that US firms have about their own sales and employment growth.

11:30 AM 2-Yr FRN Note Auction

An FRN (floating rate note) is a security that has an interest payment that can change over time.

1:00 PM 5-Yr Note Auction

Treasury notes are sold at regularly scheduled public auctions. The competitive bids at these auctions determine the interest rate paid on each Treasury note issue.

4:10 PM Stephen Miran Speaks

Federal Reserve Board Governor Stephen Miran participates in conversation before the Digital Asset Summit 2026.

data.title: Kb Home outlines shift to 70% built-to-order deliveries for stronger H2 2026 margins amid market uncertainty
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: KB Home (KBH) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Jeffrey Mezger, Executive Chairman, indicated that first quarter results were within guidance and highlighted "our highest community count in many years, contributing to year-over-year growth in net orders." He emphasized a return to a "mix of sales that are predominantly built-to-order," aiming for "70% built-to-order deliveries in the second half of this year." Mezger noted repurchasing 843,000 shares and returning almost $70 million to shareholders in the quarter.
  • Mezger announced Rob McGibney's promotion to CEO and President, stating, "Rob is a proven results-oriented leader with a deep understanding of our business gained over the past 25 years with the company."
  • Rob McGibney, CEO and President, explained, "The meaningful improvement in cancellations reflects high-quality committed buyers who are ready and able to purchase a home and also supported net orders at an average absorption pace of 3.5 per month per community." He described the shift to the built-to-order (BTO) model as a "purposeful reset that positions us to be a stronger, more predictable company in the second half of the year and beyond."
  • McGibney added, "We are making considerable progress increasing our built-to-order sales. They represented 44% of our net orders in October, growing each month through the first quarter. We exited February at 68%. And in the early weeks of March, we are now above 70%."
  • Robert Dillard, CFO, stated, "In the first quarter of fiscal 2026, we were within our guidance range with total revenues of $1.08 billion and housing revenues of $1.07 billion, a 23% decrease on a year-over-year basis." Dillard added, "We continue to manage cost effectively and achieved an 8% reduction in total direct construction costs per unit."

Outlook

  • Dillard indicated, "In the second quarter of 2026, we expect to generate housing revenues between $1.05 billion and $1.15 billion, based on expected deliveries of between 2,250 and 2,450 homes. Housing gross profit margin, assuming no inventory-related charges, is expected to be between 15% and 15.6%."
  • For the full year 2026, KB Home expects "housing revenues of between $4.8 billion and $5.5 billion based on between 10,000 and 11,500 deliveries."
  • Management expects "margins to improve in the second half of 2026, driven largely by positive operating leverage from typical seasonality and a more favorable regional mix."
  • The company plans "between $50 million and $100 million of repurchases" for the second quarter and will provide margin guidance after the spring selling season.

Financial Results

  • KB Home reported total revenues of $1.08 billion and diluted earnings per share of $0.52 for the first quarter.
  • Delivered 2,370 homes in the quarter, near the midpoint of guidance.
  • Average selling price declined 10% to $452,000 due to regional and product mix.
  • Housing gross profit margin was 15.3%, with adjusted housing gross profit margin at 15.5% after excluding $2.2 million in inventory-related charges.
  • SG&A as a percent of housing revenue increased to 12.2%. SG&A expense decreased 14% due to lower unit volume and fixed cost controls, including a notable $8 million insurance recovery.
  • Net income was $33 million, with a 13% reduction in weighted average diluted shares outstanding.

Q&A

  • Matthew Bouley, Barclays: Asked about predictability benefits of the BTO model. Rob McGibney responded, "The benefits that it provides to the business in predictability. The first place I would go is that we've got this backlog of sold not started homes that we can leverage that gives us a cadence where we can operate on even flow production."
  • Bouley: Inquired about the guidance reduction drivers. McGibney explained, "Our orders while was a positive year-over-year comp... they were below our internal expectations... the last couple of weeks have been a little softer than what we would like to see... it's weighing on the consumer."
  • Matthew Johnson, UBS: Sought color on margin trajectory. Dillard clarified, "We don't expect meaningful changes in price, but we do expect to continue to get some delivery cost reductions... the shift of ETO should accumulate to an increase in gross profit margin."
  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Asked about backlog turnover ratio. McGibney said, "I think somewhere between that 60% number and the 80% number is probably where we'll fall."
  • Alan Ratner, Zelman & Associates: Focused on pricing trends. McGibney noted, "About 70% of our communities during Q1 either had no change, or they had some level of small price increases... we did have still about 30% of our communities where we've moved price down further."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed management on guidance reductions, backlog predictability, and margin trajectory, with a slightly negative tone, frequently probing for clarity amid market uncertainty. Bouley and Ratner raised concerns about consumer demand and pricing stability.
  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing operational execution and the benefits of the BTO shift. In Q&A, McGibney acknowledged "noise out there" and uncertainty from geopolitical events, indicating a more cautious stance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management tone were more cautious, with greater emphasis on market unpredictability and guidance risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was lowered, with full year housing revenue now expected between $4.8 billion and $5.5 billion and deliveries between 10,000 and 11,500, down from the previous guidance of $5.1 billion to $6.1 billion and 11,000 to 12,500 deliveries.
  • The company accelerated its transition to a "70% built-to-order" mix for the second half, up from 57% in Q4 2025.
  • Average selling price decreased further, and margins compressed more than anticipated in Q1, with management emphasizing cost controls and regional mix for improvement in H2.
  • Analysts shifted focus from general market dynamics to specific concerns on demand softness and the impact of geopolitical risks on consumer confidence.
  • Management's confidence in the BTO model and operational execution remained, but language reflected more caution around near-term market volatility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "affordability concerns and recent geopolitical tensions" as factors impacting guidance and near-term demand.
  • The conflict in the Middle East was specifically mentioned as introducing "another layer of uncertainty" affecting consumer confidence.
  • Material costs, particularly lumber, were highlighted as a source of cost pressure, though management is "managing our lumber locks strategically."
  • Management reduced headcount by 10% year-over-year to align overhead with lower delivery volumes.
  • Analysts raised concerns about sales pace, pricing stability, and the risk of higher mortgage rates affecting backlog conversions.

Final Takeaway

KB Home management emphasized a decisive shift back to its core built-to-order strategy, targeting at least 70% BTO deliveries in the second half of 2026 to drive higher margins and predictability. While first quarter results aligned with guidance, the company lowered its full year outlook due to softer than expected orders and heightened market uncertainty, particularly from geopolitical events. Management is focusing on cost discipline, operational efficiency, and a more favorable mix, with expectations of stronger performance and margin improvement in the latter half of the year as the benefits of the BTO transition and regional mix become more pronounced.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Absci outlines $25B annual market potential for ABS-201 in AGA and advances endometriosis clinical program
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Absci Corporation (ABSI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Sean McClain reported "a strong fourth quarter," highlighting that ABS-201 is now in the clinic with the first three single ascending dose (SAD) cohorts dosed in the Phase I/IIa headline trial and "favorable emerging safety data." The company expanded into endometriosis as a second indication and published what it believes is the "first demonstration of de novo full-length antibody designed to zero-prior epitopes."
  • McClain introduced Dr. Ransi Somaratne, the new Chief Medical Officer, with registrational experience from Vertex. McClain noted, "Our development operations are in excellent hands because of the foundation [Andreas Busch] built."
  • McClain emphasized that ABS-201 was engineered for an extended half-life, with preclinical studies showing "a three to fourfold longer half-life than the competitor antibody," potentially enabling a "convenient dosing regimen of just 2 to 3 administrations for durable multiyear hair regrowth."
  • The company anticipates sharing "preliminary safety, tolerability and PK data for our ongoing headline trial in the first half of this year," with interim 13-week proof-of-concept data in the second half and full 26-week data in early 2027. ABS-201's safety, tolerability, and PK from the ongoing study will be used to support the initiation of the Phase II clinical trial in endometriosis in Q4 2026.
  • Chief Business Officer & CFO Zachariah Jonasson stated, "Revenue in the fourth quarter was $700,000 as we continue to progress our partnered programs." He added that research and development expenses rose to $25.3 million for the quarter, and the company ended the year with $144.3 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities.

Outlook

  • McClain stated, "In 2026, we expect to deliver on our catalysts, preliminary safety and PK data for ABS-201 in the first half, interim 13-week proof-of-concept hair regrowth data in the second half and initiation of our Phase II endometriosis trial in Q4, subject to data and regulatory review." The company also plans to share 26-week proof-of-concept data for ABS-201 and AGA in early 2027.
  • Jonasson reported that existing cash resources are expected to be sufficient to fund operations "into the first half of 2028."

Financial Results

  • The company reported $700,000 in revenue for the quarter. Research and development expenses were $25.3 million, while selling, general and administrative expenses were $8.6 million. A $5.1 million gain on the settlement of contingent consideration resulted in net proceeds of $8.7 million of unrestricted cash. Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of December 31, 2025, totaled $144.3 million.
  • Jonasson indicated the increase in R&D expenses was "primarily driven by advancement of Absci's internal programs, including direct costs associated with external preclinical and clinical development of ABS-101 and ABS-201."

Q&A

  • Vamil Divan, Guggenheim Securities: Asked about the target product profile for ABS-201 and efficacy expectations. McClain indicated the goal is "a durable treatment as well as being able to achieve at or above minoxidil efficacy," and Jonasson elaborated, "if the effect size in terms of terminal area hair count and the growth in terminal area hair count is consistent with high-dose oral minoxidil, so 35 to 40 hairs per square centimeter, we think that's a home run product."
  • Divan also inquired about the safety profile. Somaratne responded, "there's no evidence of any on-target or off-target safety signal based on our review of the safety data accumulated to date, but it's encouraging so far."
  • Brendan Smith, TD Cowen: Asked about the 6-month primary endpoint for registrational studies and about drug creation partnerships. Somaratne noted, "other companies are developing a 6-month pivotal endpoint with another 6 months of long-term safety data follow-up...we're going to look forward to our 13-week interim to and give you more details on that once we see the data." Jonasson stated, "we continue to have productive discussions with pharma regarding platform partnerships."
  • Lut Ming Cheng, JPMorgan: Asked about partnering ABS-101, 301, and 501, and for more color on safety. McClain confirmed 301 and 501 are better suited for partners, and Somaratne said, "there's really nothing that would point to any sort of mechanism-related safety signal...but again, I have to caveat that it's early in the study."
  • Alexander Xenakis, Truist Securities: Asked about the ability of the molecule to engage the hair follicle target. McClain explained, "based on [animal data], we have no reason to believe you wouldn't be able to get an antibody into the follicle."
  • Debanjana Chatterjee, JonesTrading: Asked about positioning relative to new competitors. McClain described ABS-201 as "a brand-new novel mechanism," and Jonasson cited survey data showing high interest among current minoxidil users for ABS-201's potential profile.
  • Gil Blum, Needham: Asked about cohort dosing, endometriosis trial challenges, and resource allocation. Somaratne discussed the complexities of pain studies and trial design, and Jonasson emphasized both AGA and endometriosis provide "significant potential ROI," with streamlined development paths.
  • Sean Laaman, Morgan Stanley: Sought clarity on AI platform differentiation. McClain pointed to "the most rigorous work that has been published to date" and the application of agentic AI for asset development. Jonasson highlighted the higher value of asset deals compared to platform deals.
  • Charles Wallace, H.C. Wainwright: Inquired about market opportunity sizing for AGA and endometriosis. McClain responded, "AGA is likely a larger opportunity...these are both very exciting opportunities from just a market size perspective." Jonasson addressed pricing, stating, "we think the pricing for both of them...we don't think there'll be an arbitrage opportunity there."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were pressing for clarity on efficacy targets, safety signals, competitive positioning, and commercial potential, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as management provided detailed responses and acknowledged early-stage limitations.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases like "we believe" and "we think that's a home run product," while repeatedly emphasizing the uniqueness and durability of ABS-201. Confidence was consistent with the previous quarter, though the addition of a new CMO brought further assurance regarding clinical execution.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone remained steady, focusing on program progress and risk management, while management's tone stayed confident and forward-looking.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the addition of Dr. Ransi Somaratne as Chief Medical Officer and the transition of Andreas Busch to Co-Chair of the Scientific Advisory Board.
  • ABS-201's clinical timeline advanced, with the first SAD cohorts now dosed and favorable emerging safety data reported, compared to the previous quarter's focus on study initiation.
  • Management continued to prioritize ABS-201 in both AGA and endometriosis, with a more explicit emphasis on commercial strategy and market size in the latest call.
  • R&D expenses increased from $19.2 million in Q3 to $25.3 million in Q4, reflecting greater clinical activity.
  • Analysts continued to focus on efficacy endpoints, safety, market positioning, and partnership progress, with more attention this quarter on differentiation and the go-to-market approach.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that ABS-201's clinical program is in early stages, repeatedly noting the need to "caveat that it's early in the study" and that safety data are "encouraging so far" but not yet comprehensive.
  • Somaratne highlighted that pain studies in endometriosis require careful site and patient selection and protocol design to mitigate placebo effects.
  • Questions from analysts touched on competitive threats from alternative therapies and the technological challenge of antibody tissue penetration.
  • Management cited ongoing engagement with the FDA and the dependence of future milestones on interim and final clinical data.

Final Takeaway

Absci closed 2025 with ABS-201 advancing in both androgenetic alopecia and endometriosis, reporting favorable emerging clinical safety and a robust cash position to support continued development. The company highlighted a potential $25 billion annual addressable market for ABS-201 in the U.S. alone and laid out a roadmap for key clinical readouts in 2026 and beyond. Management expressed confidence in the differentiated profile and commercial prospects of ABS-201 while reinforcing disciplined resource allocation and ongoing partnership discussions as central to its growth strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
